steps.title: Identify the complex list
steps.content: The sentence lists three material categories: natural debris, man-made trash, and traditional art supplies. Each category has a such as example, so the boundaries between the main list items need semicolons rather than only commas.

steps.title: Keep examples attached
steps.content: A uses a comma between list items and a colon before an example. C also uses only a comma between complex items. D wrongly separates man-made trash from its own example with a semicolon. The correct punctuation separates items while keeping each example attached. The answer is B.
